## Section B: Institutional Stakeholders — Data Sources & Referenced Organizations

| Stakeholder Name | Role | Organization Type | Responsibility / Role in Document | Mentioned Section | Source Reference |
|-----------------|------|------------------|----------------------------------|------------------|-----------------|
| **World Bank Group / International Bank for Reconstruction and Development** | Publisher; Issuing Institution | Multilateral Development Bank | Published and disseminated the report; holds copyright | Copyright Page; Throughout | Page 4 (Copyright) |
| **IEA (International Energy Agency)** | Key Data Provider | International Organization | Primary source for oil market data, gas market data, coal data, oil inventory data, and oil balance figures | Energy sections; Multiple figures | Pages 17–25; Figures 7, 8, 9, 10, 11; References |
| **OPEC+ (Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries +)** | Major Oil Supply Stakeholder | Intergovernmental Organization | Production policy decisions; OPEC+ production assumed to decrease ~5% in 2026; rebound expected later in year | Pages 5, 19, 21 | Pages 5, 19, 21 |
| **World Food Programme (WFP)** | Food Security Data Source | UN Agency | Provided projections: up to 45 million additional people in acute food insecurity under extended conflict scenario (WFP 2026 cited) | Executive Summary — Food Insecurity; Agriculture | Pages 10, 31; Figure 6.D |
| **Consensus Economics** | Private Sector Forecast Compiler | Private Research Firm | Provided March 2026 private-sector oil price consensus forecasts (10th–90th percentile range) | Energy — Oil Risks; Figure 9.A; Figure 5.A | Pages 7, 20; References |
| **U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA)** | Energy Data Provider | U.S. Government Agency | Provided oil production, natural gas, and Short-Term Energy Outlook data; April 2026 edition cited | Energy sections; Figures | Pages 20, 24; References |
| **FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations)** | Agricultural Data Source | UN Agency | Provided FAOSTAT database data on fertilizer exports and agricultural production | Agriculture; Fertilizers | Pages 29, 34; References |
| **International Fertilizer Association (IFA)** | Fertilizer Trade Data | Industry Association | Provided data on fertilizer production and trade shares by country (e.g., Middle East's ~25% share of global urea exports) | Fertilizers section | Page 34; References |
| **International Grains Council (IGC)** | Grain Market Data | International Organization | Provided grain market data including grain production changes and stock-to-use ratios | Agriculture — Grains | Pages 27–28; Figure 13; References |
| **USDA (U.S. Department of Agriculture)** | Agricultural Production Data | U.S. Government Agency | Provided Production, Supply & Distribution data; Prospective Plantings report (March 31, 2026) on U.S. crop acreage | Agriculture sections | Pages 28, 33; Figures 13.D, 17.B; References |
| **OECD (Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development)** | Economic & Energy Data | Intergovernmental Organization | Provided industrial production index; IEA member countries' strategic petroleum reserves | Special Focus; Energy | Pages 7, 50; References |
| **Oxford Economics** | GDP Growth Data | Private Research Firm | Provided GDP growth forecast data for EMDE analysis | Broader Implications; Figure 6 | Page 9; Figure 6.A note |
| **Bloomberg** | Financial Market Data | Financial Data Provider | Provided commodity price data, oil futures prices, volatility indices | Throughout all figures | Multiple figures throughout |
| **Fastmarkets** | Commodity Pricing Data | Commodity Intelligence Firm | Provided commodity price data, particularly for seaborne trade | Figures 1 | Figure 1 source notes |
| **NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)** | Climate/Weather Data | U.S. Government Agency | Provided ENSO Index data for El Niño/La Niña risk assessment | Agriculture — Risks; Figure 14.C | Page 30; Figure 14.C |
| **Gas Infrastructure Europe (AGSI+)** | EU Gas Storage Data | European Industry Association | Provided EU natural gas inventory data (20 EU countries + UK) | Natural Gas section; Figure 10.C | Page 22; Figure 10.C |
| **Caldara & Iacoviello** | Geopolitical Risk Measurement | Academic Researchers | Developed the Geopolitical Risk (GPR) Index used in the Special Focus analysis | Special Focus | Pages 49–56 |
| **OCP (Office Chérifien des Phosphates)** | Phosphate Producer | Moroccan State Company | Brought forward maintenance on phosphate production; reducing 2026Q2 output due to sulfur/ammonia disruptions | Fertilizers | Page 35, Fertilizers |

## Section C: Country / Regional Stakeholders Referenced

| Stakeholder | Role in Document | Mentioned Sections | Source Reference |
|------------|-----------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| **Middle East / Gulf Region** (Iraq, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Iran) | Primary conflict zone; dominant commodity supplier; major OPEC+ producers affected by largest supply cuts | Throughout — Energy, Fertilizers, Agriculture, Metals | Pages 1, 17–19, 34 |
| **Qatar** | Key LNG producer; production facilities (Ras Laffan) damaged; North Field expansion delayed | Natural Gas, Fertilizers | Pages 23, 34 |
| **Islamic Republic of Iran** | Ceased ammonia production; sanctions temporarily lifted on oil shipments | Energy, Fertilizers | Pages 17, 34 |
| **China** | Largest consumer of most base metals (~50%); largest aluminum producer (44 mt, near 45 mt cap); major nitrogen fertilizer producer and exporter; potential export restrictions on fertilizers | Metals, Fertilizers, Energy | Pages 5, 9, 34, 36, 38 |
| **United States** | Expected to add ~0.5 mb/d oil supply; reviewing copper import tariffs (DoC review); major biofuel mandate setter; natural gas market largely insulated from conflict | Energy, Metals, Agriculture | Pages 19–21, 40 |
| **Indonesia** | Lowered nickel ore production quotas ~30% for 2026; coal export targets being reduced; Grasberg copper mine phased restart expected | Metals, Energy | Pages 25, 37–38 |
| **Russia** | Sanctions temporarily lifted on oil shipments; gas exports to Europe fell (Ukraine transit ceased January 2025) | Energy | Pages 17, 22 |
| **República Bolivariana de Venezuela** | Temporary sanctions lifting on oil shipments to compensate for Strait disruption | Energy | Page 17 |
| **India** | Urea/ammonia production reduced due to LNG supply declines; also largest importer of both | Fertilizers | Page 34 |
| **European Union** | Gas storage at critical low levels; imposing restrictions on Russian metals imports; competing for LNG with Asian buyers | Natural Gas, Metals | Pages 22, 40 |
| **Brazil** | Sugar/ethanol switching capacity; soybean export dynamics; coffee production | Agriculture | Pages 30, 32 |
| **Lebanon** | Among most affected countries; oil consumption reduction ~30% projected | Energy | Page 19 |
| **Sub-Saharan Africa (SSA)** | High food import vulnerability; nitrogen fertilizer use per hectare fell 5% (2021) and 4% (2022); over half of the projected 45M newly food-insecure people | Agriculture, Broader Implications | Pages 10, 31 |
